policy.title: RBI finalizes climate risk disclosure framework for scheduled commercial banks
policy.description: RBI issued final guidelines requiring all scheduled commercial banks to integrate climate scenario analysis into ICAAP submissions by March 2027. Banks must report physical and transition risk exposures by sector with quantified PD/LGD impacts under at least two NGFS scenarios.

policy.title: SEBI expands BRSR Core to include Scope 3 emissions for top 250 listed companies
policy.description: SEBI amended BRSR Core requirements mandating Scope 3 value chain emissions disclosure for top 250 companies by market cap from FY2027. Reasonable assurance on climate metrics becomes mandatory, up from limited assurance.

policy.title: IRDAI releases sandbox guidelines for parametric crop and flood insurance products
policy.description: IRDAI opened a dedicated regulatory sandbox for parametric insurance with streamlined approval for products using satellite, IoT, and weather station triggers. Maximum coverage per policy raised to INR 50 crore for commercial lines.

policy.title: Basel Committee publishes final Pillar 3 climate disclosure standard
policy.description: BCBS finalized standardized disclosure tables for banks' climate-related financial risks under Pillar 3, effective January 2028. Includes mandatory metrics on financed emissions intensity by sector and concentration of exposures in high physical risk geographies.

policy.title: ISSB releases adoption guide for emerging market jurisdictions
policy.description: IFRS Foundation published implementation guidance tailored to emerging markets adopting ISSB S1/S2, with proportionality provisions for smaller entities and phased timelines. India's MCA confirmed formal endorsement process will conclude by Q3 2026.

policy.title: NGFS releases Phase V climate scenarios with enhanced physical risk granularity
policy.description: Updated NGFS scenarios now include sub-national physical hazard projections and acute event frequency distributions for South and Southeast Asia. New scenario pathways incorporate 2025 NDC updates and transition policy acceleration assumptions.

competitors.title: Jupiter Intelligence partners with HDFC Bank for physical risk scoring pilot
competitors.description: Jupiter announced a strategic pilot with HDFC Bank to integrate its ClimateScore Financial product into the bank's commercial real estate lending workflow. The engagement marks Jupiter's first major public-sector bank deal in India.

competitors.title: Sprih closes $12M Series A to expand BRSR compliance platform
competitors.description: Bangalore-based Sprih raised Series A funding led by Bertelsmann India Investments to scale its ESG reporting automation for Indian mid-market companies. The company claims 180+ enterprise clients including three PSU banks.

competitors.title: XDI launches asset-level physical risk API for Indian commercial real estate
competitors.description: XDI released India-specific modules covering flood, cyclone, and heat stress at property level, with integration into common Indian GIS formats. Pricing targets mid-size NBFCs with per-asset query model.

competitors.title: Moody's integrates physical climate risk scores into India corporate credit ratings
competitors.description: Moody's announced that physical risk assessments will now be embedded in credit opinions for Indian corporates in agriculture, real estate, and infrastructure sectors. The methodology draws on Four Twenty Seven acquisition capabilities.

competitors.title: Watershed partners with YES Bank for financed emissions measurement
competitors.description: Watershed signed a multi-year agreement with YES Bank to deploy its carbon accounting platform across the bank's corporate lending book. The deal includes PCAF-aligned Scope 3 Category 15 calculations.

competitors.title: CarbonMint launches parametric insurance underwriting data service for agri-lenders
competitors.description: CarbonMint extended its regenerative agriculture platform with a data feed for insurers designing parametric crop products, including soil moisture indices and yield deviation triggers at taluka level.

research.title: Climate Risk and Bank Capital: A Quantitative Framework for Emerging Markets
research.description: IFC study models capital adequacy impacts under physical risk scenarios for banks in India, Brazil, and Indonesia. Finds median 45-80 bps increase in PD for agriculture and real estate portfolios under RCP 4.5 by 2035.

research.title: Scaling Parametric Insurance in South Asia: Barriers and Enablers
research.description: Swiss Re analysis finds India's parametric insurance market could reach $1.2B GWP by 2030 under supportive regulation. Key barriers remain basis risk perception and lack of granular trigger data infrastructure.

research.title: ESG Data Gaps in Emerging Markets: Investor Survey 2026
research.description: Survey of 120 institutional investors reveals 73% cite lack of comparable ESG data as primary barrier to EM allocation increases. India ranked highest in data availability among surveyed EM countries but gaps persist in Scope 3 and biodiversity metrics.

research.title: Physical Climate Risk in Indian Banking: Sectoral Exposure Analysis
research.description: CRISIL estimates 18% of Indian bank lending is in sectors highly exposed to physical climate risk, with agriculture, textiles, and coastal real estate most vulnerable. Report provides district-level hazard overlays for flood and drought.

research.title: Flood Risk Financing in South Asia: The Role of Index Insurance
research.description: World Bank study finds parametric flood insurance could reduce fiscal contingent liability by 25-40% for state disaster response funds in Bihar, Assam, and Odisha. Recommends public-private risk layering structures.

research.title: Supervisory Expectations for Climate Scenario Analysis: Global Stocktake
research.description: FSB review of 24 jurisdictions finds convergence toward mandatory climate scenario analysis for large banks by 2027. India noted as moving faster than regional peers with RBI's March 2027 deadline.

ourRead.headline: India's climate risk regulatory clock is now running—and global competitors are already knocking on bank doors.
body: The past 60 days mark an inflection point: Indian financial regulators have shifted from consultation to implementation timelines on climate risk, while global platforms are aggressively piloting with top-tier Indian banks. The convergence of RBI's March 2027 ICAAP mandate, SEBI's Scope 3 push, and IRDAI's parametric sandbox creates a compressed window where banks must source capabilities they do not yet have in-house.

The RBI's finalized climate disclosure framework (policy-1) is the single most important signal for StepChange's core business. The requirement for quantified PD/LGD impacts under NGFS scenarios by March 2027 validates our entire Bet 1 thesis. The FSB stocktake (research-6) confirms India is ahead of EM peers—meaning Indian banks cannot wait for regional playbooks. We must position our scenario analysis module as the fastest path to ICAAP compliance for mid-tier banks who lack internal modeling teams.

Jupiter's HDFC pilot (comp-1) and XDI's India product launch (comp-3) signal that global climate risk analytics players see the Indian opportunity clearly. Meanwhile, Watershed's YES Bank deal (comp-5) shows enterprise carbon accounting is being solved by well-funded global players. StepChange should avoid competing head-on with Watershed on financed emissions—our differentiation is physical hazard-linked credit risk at the asset level, where Jupiter and XDI pricing remains prohibitive for NBFCs.

The Swiss Re parametric insurance report (research-2) quantifies a $1.2B addressable market by 2030 but flags granular trigger data as the binding constraint. CarbonMint's new taluka-level agri data service (comp-6) is a direct shot across our Bet 2 bow—they are bundling regenerative ag data with parametric underwriting inputs. We have stronger bank relationships; we need to accelerate trigger data infrastructure before CarbonMint locks up agri-lender pipelines.

One thing to watch: ISSB's emerging market adoption guide (policy-5) will shape whether Indian corporates report under global standards or a diluted domestic variant—this affects Bet 3 data interoperability. One action: convene a roundtable with 5-7 mid-tier bank risk heads on ICAAP scenario requirements within the next 30 days, while Jupiter's HDFC pilot is still in proof-of-concept phase and pricing is not yet benchmarked in the market.
